en.summary: This is the famous Sermon of Sha'ban (Khutbat al-Sha'baniyya), in which Prophet Muhammad (SAWW) delivered a sermon welcoming the month of Ramadan, narrated through the Golden Chain (Silsilat al-Dhahab) passing through all the Imams from al-Rida back to Imam Ali. He described Ramadan's extraordinary virtues -- that breaths in it count as glorification and sleep as worship -- and gave practical exhortations about fasting, charity, good character, family ties, and caring for orphans. The sermon culminated in a deeply emotional exchange where the Prophet wept foretelling Ali's martyrdom during Ramadan, and declared Ali's supreme station as his deputy, the proof of God over creation, and the one chosen by God for the Imamate just as the Prophet was chosen for prophethood.

key_terms.ضيافة الله: The Banquet of Allah -- a metaphor for Ramadan as a divine invitation
key_terms.الصراط: The Bridge (al-Sirat) over Hellfire on the Day of Judgment
key_terms.شقيق عاقر ناقة ثمود: The brother of the slayer of the she-camel of Thamud -- referring to Ibn Muljam, Ali's assassin
key_terms.حجة الله: The Proof of Allah -- a theological term for the divinely appointed authority
key_terms.الورع عن محارم الله: Abstaining from what God has forbidden -- identified as the best deed in Ramadan

en.seo_question: What is the famous Sermon of Sha'ban about the month of Ramadan?

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الْهَمْدَانِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ أَبِي الْحَسَنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مُوسَى الرِّضَا عَنْ أَبِيهِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ الصَّادِقِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ الْبَاقِرِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ زَيْنِ الْعَابِدِينَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ سَيِّدِ الشُّهَدَاءِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ سَيِّدِ الْوَصِيِّينَ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ خَطَبَنَا ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ فَقَالَ أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّهُ قَدْ أَقْبَلَ إِلَيْكُمْ شَهْرُ اللَّهِ بِالْبَرَكَةِ وَالرَّحْمَةِ وَالْمَغْفِرَةِ

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely Muhammad ibn Ibrahim ibn Ishaq al-Taleqani, a student of al-Saduq
narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn Ibrahim ibn Ishaq al-Taleqani

narrators.ambiguity_note: A transmitter of hadith in al-Saduq collections

narrators.name_ar: عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ فَضَّالٍ
narrators.name_en: Ali ibn al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal
narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal, prominent Kufan hadith scholar

narrators.name_en: his father (al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal)
narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faddal

narrators.name_ar: أَبُو الْحَسَنِ عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُوسَى الرِّضَا
narrators.name_en: Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Musa al-Rida
narrators.known_identity: Imam Ali ibn Musa al-Rida (AS), the eighth Imam (d. 203 AH)
Imam Musa ibn Ja'far al-Kadhim (AS), the seventh Imam (d. 183 AH)
Imam Ja'far ibn Muhammad al-Sadiq (AS), the sixth Imam (d. 148 AH)
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali al-Baqir (AS), the fifth Imam (d. 114 AH)
Imam Ali ibn al-Husayn Zayn al-Abidin (AS), the fourth Imam (d. 94 AH)

narrators.name_en: the Master of Martyrs al-Husayn ibn Ali
narrators.known_identity: Imam al-Husayn ibn Ali (AS), the third Imam (d. 61 AH)

narrators.name_ar: سَيِّدُ الْوَصِيِّينَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ
narrators.name_en: the Master of Deputies Amir al-Mu'minin Ali ibn Abi Talib
narrators.known_identity: Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS), the first Imam (d. 40 AH)

key_phrases.phrase_en: The Proof of God over His Creation
Fear the Fire, even with half a date
The brother of the slayer of the she-camel of Thamud

similar_content_hints.description: Similar sermons on the virtues and practices of Ramadan found in al-Kafi and other collections, emphasizing the gates of paradise being open and devils being chained.
Hadiths establishing Ali's station as the Prophet's successor and the Proof of God, such as the Hadith of Manzilah and the Hadith of Ghadir.
Narrations through the Golden Chain (Silsilat al-Dhahab) of Imam al-Rida going through all the Imams, found in Uyun Akhbar al-Rida.
